name: Chicken Grand Marnier
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove the skin from the chicken breasts.
2. In a saucepan, combine the Grand Marnier, apricot jam, vinegar, Worcestershire sauce, mustard, honey, and dried red pepper flakes. Simmer over medium-low heat (or put in microwave for 1 minute until the honey and jam are melted). Remove from the heat and let cool to room temperature.
3. Place the chicken breasts in a single layer in a shallow glass baking dish. Pour on the marinade and refrigerate, covered with plastic wrap, for at least 4 hours or overnight. If grilling, let the chicken stand at room temperature for half an hour before grilling (see step 3).
4. FOR BAKING: Pour off all but 3/4 cup of the marinade. Brush additional apricot jam onto the chicken. Cover loosely with foil. Bake at 350°F for 45 minutes to 1 hour, basting with marinade every 15 minutes.
5. FOR GRILLING: Prepare a charcoal fire. When the coals are glowing hot with a layer of white ash, grill the chicken breasts 6 to 8 inches from the heat. Baste the chicken with oil and marinade during grilling. Cook for 20 minutes, turning every 5 minutes.
6. Slice and serve hot or at room temperature.
